name: Razorpay-Style Payment API — JWT Secured
scenario: Secure the Payment API from Module 13 with JWT authentication. Anonymous users can check payment status. Authenticated users can create payments. Only ADMIN users can refund payments. Implement login/register endpoints. Test the full auth flow.
description: Add Spring Security with JWT to the existing Payment API. The security layer includes: AppUser entity, BCrypt password hashing, JWT token generation/validation, a JwtAuthFilter, and a SecurityFilterChain with role-based access. @WebMvcTest and @SpringBootTest tests cover all auth scenarios.

core.description: `AppUser` @Entity: id, username (unique), password (BCrypt), role (USER/ADMIN). `AppUserRepository` with findByUsername and existsByUsername.
`AppUserDetailsService` implementing UserDetailsService. Loads user from DB, throws UsernameNotFoundException if not found.
`JwtService`: generateToken(username, roles), extractUsername(token), isValid(token, UserDetails). Uses @Value for secret and expiry.
`JwtAuthFilter` (OncePerRequestFilter): extracts Bearer token, validates, sets SecurityContextHolder authentication.

`SecurityConfig`: disable CSRF, STATELESS sessions, rules — GET /api/payments/** public (payment status is public), POST /api/payments requires USER/ADMIN, POST /api/payments/{id}/refund requires ADMIN, /api/auth/** public.
`AuthController`: POST /api/auth/register (BCrypt hash, save user, return JWT), POST /api/auth/login (authenticate, return JWT).
@WebMvcTest tests (5): GET payment public 200, POST payment no auth 401, POST payment with @WithMockUser 201, POST refund as USER 403, POST refund as ADMIN 200.

bonus.description: Add @SpringBootTest integration test: register user, login, use token to create payment, verify in DB, attempt refund as USER (403), create ADMIN user, login as ADMIN, refund succeeds (200).
Add token refresh endpoint: POST /api/auth/refresh with valid (non-expired) token → returns new token with extended expiry. Reject expired tokens.
Add @PreAuthorize at method level: `@PreAuthorize('hasRole("ADMIN") or #userId == authentication.name')` on getPaymentsByUser. Enables @EnableMethodSecurity in SecurityConfig.

data_to_use:
Test users:
- username=aisha, password=Secure123!, role=USER
- username=admin, password=Admin123!, role=ADMIN

Security rules:
- GET /api/payments/** → public (check payment status without login)
- POST /api/payments → USER or ADMIN (create payment)
- POST /api/payments/{id}/refund → ADMIN only
- /api/auth/** → public (login and register)

jwt.secret: use openssl rand -base64 32 to generate
jwt.expiration: 86400000 (24 hours)
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. JWT secret: base64-encode a 32-byte random value (openssl rand -base64 32). BCryptPasswordEncoder: @Bean in SecurityConfig, injected where needed. STATELESS session: mandatory. @WithMockUser needs @MockBean for JwtService and UserDetailsService since JwtAuthFilter references them.
submission_instructions: Push to GitHub. Include all 7 files listed in starter_code. Screenshot showing: successful login response with token, successful payment creation with token, 401 without token, 403 for USER trying to refund.
tips_for_success: Generate secret: openssl rand -base64 32 → paste in application.properties
BCryptPasswordEncoder: declare as @Bean in SecurityConfig, inject everywhere
Register: passwordEncoder.encode(req.password()) BEFORE saving to DB
Token extraction: substring(7) removes 'Bearer ' (7 chars including space)
